Description: Route 53 DNS configuration for ip6research.com pointing to CloudFront distribution.

HostedZoneName.Description: The domain name for the hosted zone.

CloudFrontDistributionDomainName.Description: The domain name of the CloudFront distribution (e.g., d111111abcdef8.cloudfront.net). Obtain this from the CloudFront console after creating the distribution.

CloudFrontHostedZoneId.Description: The hosted zone ID for CloudFront distributions. This is always Z2FDTNDATAQYW2 for CloudFront.

Properties._Comment: IPv4 alias record pointing the apex domain to CloudFront
IPv6 alias record pointing the apex domain to CloudFront
IPv4 alias record pointing www subdomain to CloudFront
IPv6 alias record pointing www subdomain to CloudFront

HostedZoneId.Description: The ID of the hosted zone. Use this when configuring domain nameservers.

NameServers.Description: The nameservers assigned to this hosted zone. Update your domain registrar with these values.

_ManualSteps: 1. If the domain is registered outside AWS, update the registrar's nameservers to match the Route 53 hosted zone NS records.
2. If transferring the domain to Route 53, initiate the transfer from the Route 53 console and confirm via the registrant email.
3. DNS propagation can take up to 48 hours after nameserver changes.
4. The CloudFront distribution must be fully deployed before DNS records will resolve correctly.
5. Ensure the ACM certificate covers both ip6research.com and www.ip6research.com before associating with CloudFront.
